key_insight: A multi-level neural network that adds one stage per frequency can progressively reconstruct higher-order Fourier components of an inverse scattering target.
engineering_relevance: The framework turns a hard global inverse problem into a sequence of simpler frequency-by-frequency learning tasks, improving robustness and training stability for imaging applications.
method: Build the network along the frequency axis, adding a new level at each frequency.
Use each frequency level to refine the reconstruction and capture progressively finer Fourier modes.
Analyze behavior in the neural tangent kernel regime to explain why higher frequencies recover higher-order structure.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on both inverse source scattering and inverse medium scattering, along with theoretical NTK analysis supporting progressive recovery.
watch_item: Performance may depend on having sufficiently rich multi-frequency data and careful level-wise training so the staged model does not miss fine-scale features.
paper_title: A Multi-Level Machine Learning Framework for Inverse Scattering Problems with Multi-Frequency Data
